description: Migration creates tenant MSP SSO login-domain persistence model with expected columns.
Migration rollback removes tenant MSP SSO login-domain persistence objects cleanly.
Schema includes indexes supporting fast lookup by normalized domain and tenant domain listing.
List login-domain action denies unauthorized users and client users.
List login-domain action returns normalized, deduplicated tenant domains.
Save login-domain action persists valid domains for the tenant.
Save login-domain action lowercases and trims domains before persistence.
Save login-domain action rejects malformed domains with a deterministic validation error.
Save login-domain action prevents duplicate domains in one tenant payload.
Cross-tenant domain conflict behavior follows configured policy (reject or mark ambiguous).
Removing/deactivating a tenant login domain updates subsequent listing and discovery reads.
Providers settings page renders MSP SSO login-domain management section.
Providers UI add-domain flow invokes save action and refreshes rendered domain list.
Providers UI remove-domain flow invokes save action and removes domain row from view.
Providers UI shows malformed-domain validation errors without exposing backend internals.
Providers UI shows conflict/ambiguity error state with neutral language.
Discovery endpoint returns `{ ok: true, providers: [] }` for invalid email input.
Discovery endpoint normalizes email and extracts domain correctly from mixed-case input.
Discovery endpoint rate-limited calls return the same neutral response schema.
Known mapped domain with tenant Microsoft configured returns only `azure-ad`.
Known mapped domain with both tenant providers configured returns `google` and `azure-ad`.
Known mapped domain with no tenant providers configured returns empty providers list.
Unresolved domain with app Google fallback configured returns only `google`.
Unresolved domain with app Microsoft fallback configured returns only `azure-ad`.
Unresolved domain with no app fallback providers configured returns empty provider list.
Discovery implementation contract does not branch on specific-user existence lookup results.
Discovery logs avoid raw email and include only safe domain/hash metadata.
Discovery context cookie is signed and excludes OAuth client IDs/secrets.
Discovery context cookie expires according to configured short TTL.
Discovery endpoint rotates cookie on valid requests and clears stale context on invalid input.
MSP SSO buttons remain disabled for invalid/empty email input.
MSP SSO buttons remain disabled while discovery request is in flight.
MSP login enables only Microsoft button when discovery returns `azure-ad` only.
MSP login enables both buttons when discovery returns both providers.
MSP login keeps unsupported provider buttons disabled based on discovery response.
Last-selected provider preference is persisted locally when user completes provider click.
Remembered provider is only auto-selected when it is still present in discovered provider list.
Clicking a disabled provider button never triggers resolver/start API call.
Resolver consumes valid discovery cookie and uses tenant/source metadata for provider start.
Resolver rejects provider attempts not included in discovered allowed provider set using generic failure response.
Resolver falls back to app-level behavior when discovery cookie is missing, invalid, or expired.
Unknown-user and known-user paths remain externally indistinguishable in resolver responses.
Resolver rate-limit failures preserve the same generic response shape and wording.
Resolver logging excludes raw email and other sensitive identifiers.
OAuth callback flow for unknown users remains unchanged (no discovery-specific account-existence messaging).
MSP credentials sign-in flow remains functional and independent from SSO discovery outcome.
Client portal sign-in flow remains unchanged with no MSP discovery behavior bleed-through.
CE/EE SSO component wiring continues to route MSP login through shared discovery-enabled SSO entrypoint.
DB-backed integration happy path: mapped tenant domain + tenant Microsoft secrets yields discovery providers `["azure-ad"]`.
DB-backed integration guard path: ambiguous duplicate domain mapping resolves as unresolved and returns neutral provider set.
DB-backed integration guard path: inactive/deleted domain mappings are ignored by discovery.
Documentation contract includes tenant login-domain setup in provider configuration instructions.
Environment/docs contract explains unresolved-domain app-fallback provider behavior.
Route contract verifies `/auth/msp/signin` path remains unchanged after discovery rollout.
Callback URL passthrough remains intact for MSP login redirects when SSO discovery is active.
Backfill migration populates initial login-domain entries from tenant primary email domain only when unambiguous.
Backfill migration skips conflicting candidate domains and records deterministic no-op behavior.
CE and EE both expose discovery route + resolver gating behavior with identical external API contracts.
